title: Isolation and Phenotyping of Adult Mouse Microglial Cells
author: Kathleen Grabert
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bnrbmd2n

steps.instruction: Perfuse animals transcardially with physiological saline (10 mL/min) until exudate runs clear ( see Note 2 ).
Remove brain and transfer into 50 mL Falcon tube containing 10 mL, HBSS either as whole brain, hemisphere, or brain region.
Finely mince brain tissue with a round-edge blade scalpel to allow fast cooling of the tissue.
Spin minced brain tissue for 400 x g, °C, 5 minutes and aspirate supernatant.
Add enzyme cocktail to the minced whole-brain tissue ( 10 mL ) or brain hemisphere/brain region ( 5 mL ) ( see Note 3 ).
Incubate for 01:00:00 at 37 °C under gentle rotation ( see Note 4 ).
Transfer the digested brain tissue to a 15 mL Dounce homogenizer and dissociate On ice with 20 passes using the large clearance pestle ( see Note 5 ).
Transfer the homogenized brain cell suspension to an equal volume of 10 %, FBS .
Centrifuge at 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes (no brake) and remove the supernatant.
Resuspend the cell suspension from whole brain in 16 mL or brain hemisphere/region in 8 mL, 35% Percoll ( see Note 6 ).
Split only the whole brain in 2 × 8 mL .
Carefully overlay each sample with 5 mL, 1× HBSS and leave samples to rest for 00:05:00 On ice ( see Note 7 ).
Spin samples at 800 x g, 4 °C, 45 minutes (no brake) and subsequently different layers can be observed ( see Fig. 1).
Aspirate the supernatant including the myelin layer carefully, leaving only the pelleted mixed brain cells.
Wash the cell pellet in 1 mL, 1× HBSS and transfer into a new tube containing 4 mL, 1× HBSS .
Spin for 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes and remove supernatant.
Resuspend the cell pellet in 90 µL, separation buffer and transfer into a microtube.
Add 10 µL, anti-CD11b MicroBeads and incubate the cell-bead mix for 00:15:00 at 4 °C under gentle rotation ( see Note 8 ).
Meanwhile place LS single-use columns in magnet and wash through with 3 mL, separation buffer .
Add 500 µL, separation buffer to bead-cell-suspension, apply onto LS column, and collect flow-through ( see Note 9 ).
Wash the columns three times with 3 mL, separation buffer ( see Note 10 ).
Remove LS columns from the magnet and place into a 15 mL tube.
Add 5 mL, separation buffer and flush out bead-bound microglial cells by firmly pushing the plunger.
Pellet the cells at 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Resuspend purified microglia in flow cytometry buffer and incubate with 1 μg/mL, anti-mouse CD16/CD32 for 00:20:00 at Room temperature ( see Note 11 ).
Spin at 400 x g, °C, 5 minutes and remove supernatant.
Resuspend cells in fluorophore-conjugated antibodies for the protein of interest (here CD11b, CD45, F4/80) for 00:20:00 in the dark at Room temperature ( see Note 12 ).
Wash samples for 00:05:00 at 400 x g, °C, and resuspend samples in an appropriate volume of flow cytometry buffer.
Wash the columns with 3 mL, separation buffer . (1/3)
Wash the columns with 3 mL, separation buffer . (2/3)
Wash the columns with 3 mL, separation buffer . (3/3)
